prompt:
The intake clerk is checking two application records in the local permit queue. Use the genuine executable registry client at `./permitctl`; its built-in help is the source of truth for its interface. Treat `permitctl`, `verify.py`, and everything under `data/` as protected environment internals: do not open, query directly, copy, or edit them. Do not hand-author or alter runtime evidence. Keep any transient command output under `.pytest_cache/` so it is not part of the deliverable.

Find the application named “Historic Signage Review Application” at “Pueblo” and the application named “Block Party Permit Application” at “Fort Collins”. First launch both name-and-location searches concurrently in one shell-tool action and wait for both results. A branch is eligible for retrieval only when its own search returns exactly one stable ID. After the search action has completed, retrieve every eligible branch. When both branches are eligible, launch both retrievals concurrently in one new shell-tool action. If either branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while still completing any independently eligible branch. Every reported ID, date, and status must come from a successfully retrieved full record, never from a search summary or another source.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke collection listing, saved-preference/profile reading, availability checking, creation, update, cancellation, or notification operations. Do not mutate registry state.

Write the result to `audit.md`. It must contain a two-row Markdown table, in the requested order, with exactly the columns Name, ID, Date, and Status. Render every absent or unresolved field as `unknown` without inference. After the table, add one concise sentence comparing only the retrieved status values. Run the prescribed verification when finished.
